event_info: starts off with a short ball outside off; Quinton de Kock rides the bounce and taps it towards backward point. He was keen for a single; but Malewar wasn't. Sends him back
tad short outside off; Quinton de Kock taps it with soft hands towards short third man and scampers across to the other end
that's a lovely delivery by Mohammed Siraj. Back of a length delivery outside off; shaping away from the batter; Danish Malewar looks to defend; gets beaten due to the away movement
on a length around off; Danish Malewar opens the bat face; steering this through short third man for one
good length delivery outside off; Quinton de Kock knocks it towards point from within the crease
short and wide outside off; Quinton de Kock slashes away from his body; connects thin air though
on a length around off; Danish Malewar opens the bat face; steering this away for another single

that's the kind of form he is in. Can't afford to bowl such looseners against him. Kagiso Rabada fires a full length delivery on middle and leg; Quinton de Kock picks the length early and flicks it over deep mid-wicket for the first maximum of the evening
fraction short outside off; too close to the body. Quinton de Kock goes for a cut; gets an inside edge towards Buttler. A good stop by him behind the wickets
pitched up by the bowler outside off; Quinton de Kock drives it towards wide mid-off and scampers across to the other end

ball_event: out Lbw!! Kagiso Rabada strikes in his over. That's not a great start for Danish Malewar. He departs early on his MI debut. Kagiso Rabada attacks the stumps with this length delivery. Danish Malewar shuffles across in the crease to play it across the line
event_info: but gets beaten for pace. Rapped on the pads and the umpire reckons it would've crashed into the stumps. Looked plumb to the naked eye and the batter doesn't bother reviewing it. Doesn't waste any time and starts walking back to the pavilion. Danish Malewar lbw b Kagiso Rabada 2(4)
steered towards backward point for a single
drifting on the pads; clipped away through the on-side for one
good length delivery around middle and leg; worked through the leg-side

easy pickings for Naman Dhir. This was drifting on the pads and the in-form batter put it away to the fine leg fence with a well-executed flick. No need to run for those. Immediately a short fine leg in place by Siraj

back-to-back boundaries. On a length around off; it shapes back in slightly as Naman Dhir looks to defend it. Off the inside edge past the fielder at short fine leg. There was a fielder in the deep as well; but he was a tad square to cut this down
on a length outside off; Naman Dhir punches it crisply towards cover. Shahrukh Khan stationed there dives to his left and grabs it with one hand
10 runs from the over; that's quite productive for MI. This was a length delivery as Naman Dhir nudged it towards mid-wicket
good length delivery outside off; Quinton de Kock goes for a drive; gets it off the inside half of his blade towards mid-wicket

cut away! Width on offer outside off; Quinton de Kock throws the kitchen sink at it; carving it away through backward point for four more. Has hit a couple of fine shots against Kagiso Rabada already in this innings

ball_event: out Caught&Bowled!! Kagiso Rabada has the last laugh. Good pace on that delivery. Almost 150 clicks
event_info: 149.7 kph to be precise. This was a short and quick delivery around off; and Quinton de Kock tries to play this across the line. Hacks across the line; loses his shape in the process and the top edge has been taken by Rabada. Not for the first time he has been dismissed while playing this shot. This shot has resulted in his downfall several times before. Kagiso Rabada has gotten rid of the openers within the powerplay itself. He has been in fantastic rhythm this season and making his form count. Quinton de Kock c and b Kagiso Rabada 13(11) [4s-1 6s-1]
on a length around middle and leg; Suryakumar Yadav defends it down the pitch
fires this down the leg-side; Suryakumar Yadav fails to glance it away
back of a length around off; Suryakumar Yadav knocks it towards mid-off from within the crease
back of a length outside off; Suryakumar Yadav runs it down to third man
drifting on the pads; nudged through mid-wicket for a single

1 run; half-hearted appeal for an lbw; but I think this was sliding down the leg-side. A length delivery around leg; goes on with the angle and strikes him on the pad. Would have missed leg; hence the umpire wasn't interested in the appeal
on a length around off; Suryakumar Yadav chips it on the bounce to cover
1 run; yet another leg bye as the ball clips Naman Dhir's thigh pad and rolls towards short fine
on a length around off; Suryakumar Yadav knocks it down the pitch for a single
fraction short on middle and leg; worked towards mid-wicket by Naman Dhir
top-notch shot. Kagiso Rabada goes full and he drifts in line as well; Suryakumar Yadav stays put and flicks it over deep square leg for half-a-dozen. Typical SKY. That was a clean shot
now he goes straight down the ground. Kagiso Rabada goes full again and Suryakumar Yadav drills it past Kagiso Rabada for four runs. Couldn't have played it any straighter
tad short around off; Suryakumar Yadav looks to defend; gets an inside edge onto the pads
back of a length around off; Naman Dhir knocks it towards cover for one

ball_event: out Bowled!! Three wickets for Kagiso Rabada in the powerplay. He's breathing fire today. This was bowled at 152.1 kph and the stumps have been shattered. This was pitched up slightly and it jagged in a touch. Suryakumar Yadav was beaten for pace I guess as he went for a drive. Right through his gates
event_info: the furniture is disturbed; and SKY's poor run continues. Played a couple of good looking shots; but Kagiso Rabada is on a roll. Has been an outstanding spell by him already. Mumbai Indians on the back foot pretty early in the innings. They would need a special effort from the batters to follow next. Suryakumar Yadav b Kagiso Rabada 15(10) [4s-1 6s-1]
Tilak Varma is off the mark with a couple of runs; clips this through the on-side and gets going
147.3kph; short and quick on middle and leg; Naman Dhir looks to whip this away; gets hit on the thigh pad
150kph; on a length around middle and leg; knocked down the pitch by Naman Dhir
pitched up by the bowler around off; driven crisply to covers
149.9kph; Ashok Sharma goes full and Naman Dhir creams it towards cover

147.5kph; tad short outside off; Naman Dhir transfers his weight onto the backfoot and plays a late cut; carving this away to the deep backward point boundary. 50 up for Mumbai Indians in the 7th over

back-to-back boundaries for Naman Dhir! This was on the shorter side again; Naman Dhir just like the previous delivery; plays it late again. Steers it towards third man where Rabada's effort is not effort to save the boundary. Started the over on a good note; but concedes two boundaries to cap it off
flatter through the air on off; knocked down the pitch to long-off
nudged towards long-on for one by Naman Dhir
90.6kph; flatter and quicker through the air; Tilak Varma gently nudges it through backward square leg
95.5kph; googly that keeps low; Naman Dhir nudges it towards mid-wicket
touch short on middle and leg; was a googly that didn't turn to that extent. Naman Dhir guides it back to the spinner
driven through the off-side for one. Just 4 runs from that over
on a length around leg; Naman Dhir clips it through the leg-side for one
back of a length outside off; punched through the off-side for two runs
141.6kph; good length delivery outside off; knocked to cover
143 clicks; Tilak Varma plays a crisp off-drive; but Gill stationed at mid-off dives to his left and saves a certain boundary

144.5kph; Prasidh Krishna goes full on off and Naman Dhir creams it past the fielder at mid-off. The fielder with a dive yet again; but he couldn't stop it this time around. This was played a lot straighter; hence Gill couldn't get there
plays this towards cover and takes a quick single. An overthrow at the non-striker's end; results in the second run
goes full on middle and leg; driven to long-on by Tilak Varma
1 run; googly that keeps really low; Naman Dhir tries to drag it through the leg-side; gets hit on the pads though. Sliding down the leg-side
1 run; played through the on-side for a single by Tilak Varma
another short ball that keeps low; Naman Dhir drags it through the on-side for one
round the wicket angle on this delivery; Tilak Varma clips it off an inside edge towards the on-side
clipped towards deep mid-wicket where a fumble by the fielder (Manav Suthar) results in the second run. He was charging towards the ball to gather it; but couldn't do so
clipped through the on-side by Tilak Varma for one
that's a clean strike by Naman Dhir. Flatter through the air around middle and leg; Naman Dhir picks the bones out of it; launching it over long-on for a towering six
smashed past the bowler. That was tad short and Naman Dhir thumped it over Washington Sundar. Bisects a couple of fielders in the deep and that's a boundary more
quicker through the air on middle and leg; clipped towards long-on by Naman Dhir
short and quick on off; guided back to the spinner by Tilak Varma
full and straight outside off; pushed towards cover-point by Tilak Varma
spinning into the left-hander from round the wicket; Tilak Varma eases it to long-on for one
sharp leg-break; Naman Dhir pats it into the off-side for one
good running between the wickets; Tilak Varma clips it through backward square leg and takes a couple of runs
on the fuller side; Tilak Varma nudges it away; back to Rashid Khan
short in length as Tilak Varma clips it through the leg-side
50-run stand up. Clipped towards long-on for a couple of runs to end the over
advances down the wicket and pats this down the pitch
dragged towards long-on by Tilak Varma

ball_event: out Caught by Kagiso Rabada!! Prasidh Krishna breaks the partnership. He has taken all his wickets in this phase. Yet another big wicket for him. Gets the set batter - Naman Dhir. Goes short and aims it the body
event_info: Naman Dhir swivel-pulls it towards deep square leg; but doesn't get the timing right. Rabada from the deep charges in and completes a good low catch. Naman Dhir c Kagiso Rabada b Prasidh Krishna 45(32) [4s-6 6s-1]
down the wicket to knock this towards mid-off for a single. He's off the mark straightaway
short ball at the hips; tucked away through the leg-side by Tilak Varma
interesting shot by Hardik Pandya. Charges down the wicket and tries to drag this through the on-side; gets an inside edge onto the pads. The ball dribbles towards Prasidh Krishna
change of ends for Sharma; full and just outside off; Varma squeezes it straight to the fielder at point
banged in short; this one hurried onto him; Varma mistimes a pull to mid-wicket
1 run; slower short delivery outside off; Pandya leaves it alone; but it too slow as Butler couldn't stop it on the bounce
he's mixing the pace well here; pacy delivery; short and targeting the body; Varma slaps it to long-on; couldn't get the timing right
overpitched and outside off; Pandya gets under the ball nicely and squeezes it square of the wicket for a couple
he's beaten for an extra pace; back of a length and angling in sharply; this one hurried onto him; Pandya was hanging back in the crease and is cut into halves as the ball whizzes over the stumps
nice clean hit; slower and fuller; right in the arc; Varma gets hold of it and hits it through the line to long-off; the mid off was up in the circle and he takes full toll of it
this is cheeky; on a length and on middle; Varma moves across to the off side and paddles it over the fielder to fine leg; innovation at its best!
he's taking Krishna to the cleaners; length delivery outside off; width on offer; Varma hammers it to sweeper cover; he's picking the gap to perfection
fuller length outside off; Varma comes down the track and drives it to sweeper cover
banged in short; way over the batsman; he's to bowl that again
MI lose the review. Waist high full-toss; Varma was surprised by the delivery as he tried to play it to the off side; the ball goes off the edge to third man
back of a length and outside off ; Pandya taps it towards covers for a single; 19 off the over
loses his shape while playing this shot; ends up lofting it over covers for two
tad short outside off; Pandya goes for a cut and misses
beaten outside off by the short and slow ball
tries to run this towards third man; misses
advances down the wicket to knock this towards long-on
banged in short on middle; Varma mistimes a pull to backward square leg; this was bowled slower
massive hit; he's hit straight over the bowler's head; tossed up nicely wide of the left hander; Varma pounces on it and hits it through the line over long off

tad short and outside off; the googly; Varma goes back and cuts it away to sweeper cover; Phillips had other plans; chases it well and puts in a difficult dive and almost pulled it off; but the ball has the last laugh as it brushes the ropes
tossed up on middle; Varma pulls it to deep mid-wicket
quicker and fuller on middle; Pandya pushes it down to long-on
tossed up on middle; Varma lofts it in the air to deep mid-wicket; luckily it fell in the vacant region
a rare misfield from Phillips; length on middle; Pandya works it away to mid-on; a fumble and they get back for the second

slapped away; short and outside off; enough width on offer; Varma gets on top of it and sways it over deep backward point; it sailed over the fence; the partnership between Tilak Varma and Hardik Pandya is now 50
he's toying with the bowling; short and outside off;; from round the wicket this time; Varma moves across to the off side and scoops it past the fielder to fine leg
the mid-off is up in the circle and he's placed it well; full this time and outside off; actually it was a mistimed drive; but he had hit it in the gap past the fielder to long-off
this is the over they were targeting; in the slot; not an ideal ball to bowl; Varma was waiting for and dispatches it way into the stands over deep mid-wicket

he's dealing in sixes at the moment; speared in full on middle; Varma was rooted to the crease; gets underneath the ball nicely and hits it over long-off; the fielder was at the edge of the ropes; but it just sailed over him

that must be painful; he's been hit on the box and Varma is down on the ground; that was bowled at 150KPH; short and on middle; Varma goes for another cross batted short; gets an inside edge onto his body
one bounce and into the fence; the mid off was up in the circle; it was a short delivery outside off; Pandya was hanging back in the crease and flat-bats it over the fielder to long-off
GT lose the review. Good comeback from Siraj; perfect yorker on middle; Pandya just manages to get some bat to it and the ball rolls off the inside edge
spot on yorker on middle; Varma has somehow managed to put it away; he clears his right leg and smashes it straight past the bowler to long-on
full again; this time no width; Varma digs it down to mid-on

ball_event: out Caught by Glenn Phillips!! Breakthrough
event_info: low full-toss on middle; Pandya clears his left leg and flicks it in the air towards deep mid wicket; got off the toe-end of the bat and finds Phillips in the deep; he was struggling to time the ball tonight. Hardik Pandya c Glenn Phillips b Mohammed Siraj 15(16) [4s-1]
excellent delivery; yorker just outside off; Rutherford fails to dig it out; excellent finish to the over
he's found a liking to Krishna tonight; full and on middle; Varma was looking to go inside out; but slices it over the fielder at point towards third man

this is excellent batting; his highest score in the IPL; slower short delivery on the stumps; Varma was waiting for it as he dispatches it over the fielder to deep mid-wicket; it just sailed over Rabada in the deep
speared in full and outside off; Varma digs it down to long-on; call was for two; but they settle for one
low full-toss outside off; Rutherford slices it square of the wicket; nicely bowled
he's put that off in style; juicy full-toss on the stumps; worst delivery; Varma was down on one knee; picks it and scoops it over fine leg; unbelievable innings!

maiden hundred for Tilak in the IPL; this is a special knock; slower short delivery; Varma has played the cross-batted shot well throughout the innings; this time hammers it to deep square leg; found and gap and he celebrates in style. Hardik leads the celebrations - jumping up and down while clapping. Varma removes his helmet; puts it down with the bat and then points his left forefinger skywards and the right forefinger to the side of his forehead. He then folds his hands and looks towards the heavens. He is pumped and so are the rest of the Mumbai Indians

ball_event: out Caught by Krish Bhagat!! First wicket for Bumbrah this season
event_info: massive strike for MI; full and it moved away a bit; Sudharsan's feet didn't move as he looks to slice it over covers; but gets it off the toe-end of the bat and Krish stays on the edge of the circle near cover-point and takes a comfortable catch; early setback for GT as the in-form Sudharsan departs. Sai Sudharsan c Krish Bhagat b Jasprit Bumrah 0(1)
he's squared him up; on a length on middle and shaping away; Buttler was rooted to the crease to defend; the ball goes off the outer half of the bat towards the off side
he's on song at the moment; on a length outside off and moving away; Buttler leaves it alone
outside off and angling in; Buttler defends it to the off side; tails up for Bumrah
length and slight away movement; Buttler opens the bat face and guides it down to deep backward point
excellent over to start with; angling in from a length; targeting the stumps; Gill gets right behind the ball and defends it
excellent shot; that is glorious; overpitched and in the slot; little bit too wide; Buttler eases into it and hits it straight through the line to deep extra cover

ball_event: out Lbw!! Another one bites the dust
event_info: this one nipped back in from a length; Buttler was rooted to the crease and plays down the wrong line and is struck on the pads; initially it looked like it would miss the leg stump; but replays suggest it was clipping it; GT lose a review. Jos Buttler lbw b Hardik Pandya 5(6) [4s-1]
back of a length and outside off; Sundar leaves it alone
poor delivery; on a length and drifting down leg; Sundar just had to get some bat to it and it raced away to fine leg fence
short and angling away; Sundar leaves it alone
very good hit; full and on off; Sundar strides forward; presents the full face of the bat and just hits it on the rise to long-off
this is poor bowling; angling way down the leg side and a good take from the keeper

he finishes the over with a boundary; expensive from Pandya; back of a length and outside off; too much width on offer; Sundar rocks back and smashes it past the fielder to square of the wicket; that one raced away!
angling in from outside off; bit too high; Gill plays across the line and is hit high on the pads
angling down the leg side; Gill misses out on the clip shot
glorious! full and in the slot; little bit of room; Gill doesn't miss out on those; reaches out to it and creams it through sweeper cover; the sweet sound of the bat as it went off the middle
that just skidded through; on a length and just outside off; it moved away and kept a bit low as Gill missed it completely
right on the money; angling into the stumps; Gill gets forward and defends it towards the off side
four more; slightly shorter and outside off; Gill gets on top of it and cuts it away square of the wicket; that was a cracking shot from the classy Gill
back of a length and it nipped back in sharply; Gill was taken aback as he was looking to defend; the ball goes off the inside edge to backward square leg; two was the call; but only a single
Krish on debut; bowls it full just outside off; Gill presents full face and drives it straight to the fielder at mid off
back of a length and angling in; Gill shimmies down the track and works it to deep square leg; he was cramped for any room
this one kept low; length and outside off; Sundar pokes at it and misses
back of a length and angling across; Sundar whips it to mid-wicket; a fumble; but they only get a single
stand and deliver; over-pitched and in the slot; Gill doesn't miss out on that; high elbow as he lofts it through the line to deep extra cover
this one nips back in; Gill whips it to short midwicket
he starts off with a boundary; drifting on the leg stump with fine leg up in the circle is a crime; Sundar was rooted to the crease and whips it off his hips to backward square leg
back of a length and outside off; Sundar slaps it to backward point
back of a length and shaping in; good delivery; Gill gets right behind the line and defends it

banged in short on the stumps; Gill was not in a position to play the whip shot; he had moved across to the leg side; but went through with the shot and finds Naman in the deep; who didn't have to move an inch to take the catch.MI all over GT at the moment. Shubman Gill c Naman Dhir b Ashwani Kumar 14(13) [4s-3]
back of a length and outside off; Phillips stands tall and punches it to sweeper cover
back of a length and outside off; little bit of away movement; Sundar pushes it to the off side
back of a length and angling in; Phillips works it to mid-wicket for a quick single
pitched outside leg stumps and angling across; Sundar misses out on the clip shot and sets off for a single; sent back
full and on middle; Sundar flicks it to deep square leg
angling in from outside off; Phillips works it away to mid-wicket; he hit that in the gap and they come back comfortably for the second
nipping back in on a length; Phillips pushes it towards the off side
full and targeting the stumps; Phillips drives it down on the track; half stop by the bowler
flatter and fraction short; Sundar uses his wrists and carves it past backward point; easy brace

uhhh! Skidding in from round the wicket; it was the carrom ball and it kept coming in; Sundar prods forward and is beaten on the inside-edge. Just goes over the stumps and de Kock does not glove it cleanly
carrom ball that's a touch fuller; Sundar pushes it towards cover
streaky! Slows it up and it is the carrom ball again; Sundar crouches low and looks for the slog sweep; gets an inside-edge and the ball escapes past the stumps. That wasn't far..
97.6ks fuller and around off; Sundar closes the bat face early and the leading edge rolls to long-off
92.8ks flighted delivery; Phillips drives it down to long-on and retains strike
87ks short of length and drifting in; Phillips knocks it to long-on

ball_event: out Caught by Naman Dhir!! That's a terrific catch. Santner strikes in his first over. Gujarat Titans slip further. That was not a wicket-taking delivery but Dhir has done Santner a favour. Dragged short
event_info: Sundar rocks back and pulls it towards deep mid-wicket. The ball seemed to be sailing over but not on Dhir's watch - he times his leap to perfection; takes it over his head and lands inside the rope. He balances himself and ensures he does not step on the ad cushions. A quick check by the third umpire and it is deemed a clean catch. Washington Sundar c Naman Dhir b Mitchell Santner 26(17) [4s-5]
85.4ks tossed up on leg; Tewatia nurdles it to deep square leg

ball_event: out Caught&Bowled!! Gone! Second wicket in the over and Santner has the better of his fellow New Zealand team mate. Again slower through the air
event_info: the ball does not quite come on and Phillips is hanging back; looks to punch it down the ground but is early into the shot; chips it to the right of the bowler; Santner dives across and gobbles it up with both hands. Gujarat Titans in tatters with half their side back in the hut. Glenn Phillips c and b Mitchell Santner 6(8)
80ks flighted outside off; Shahrukh eases it wide of cover and gets off the mark
79.5ks tossed up close to off; Tewatia gently turns it to mid-wicket
100.4ks quicker and sliding in; Shahrukh defends it back to Ghazanfar
100.3ks carrom ball that grips and straightens; Shahrukh watchfully blocks to cover
100ks drifting in with the angle; Shahrukh hurriedly brings his bat down and knocks it to mid-wicket
99ks flatter and skidding in; Shahrukh eases it to long-on
98.8ks sliding in; Tewatia plays it with soft hands to the off-side and quickly crosses over

just over long-off. Shahrukh decides to take on Ghazanfar - comes down the track; gets a bit too close to the ball and ends up slicing the loft. But the power he possess takes it over the leaping fielder
90.9ks shortens the length around off; Tewatia rides the bounce and knocks it to cover
comes down the track and does not get to the pitch; works it to square leg and wants a single. Shahrukh is not interested and is quick to send Tewatia back
78.4ks slower and turning in; Tewatia helps it round the corner - backward square leg
88.1ks full pitcher on off; Shahrukh pushes it back firmly and Santner makes a diving stop
91ks fuller and close to off; Shahrukh drives it towards cover and Hardik dives to his right; gets a hand and takes the pace off; just a couple

that's a gift and Shahrukh takes advantage. Overcooks the flighted delivery and offers a friendly full toss; Shahrukh drives it in front of square and this time neither Hardik nor the sweeper had a chance to stop that
Tewatia needs to get going as well. He picks the full delivery; sits down and belts the sweep in front of square; beating deep mid-wicket and deep square leg comfortably

he was shaping for another swipe; but the length was pulled back and Tewatia adjusts quickly; brings his bat across and stabs it towards mid-on. He got into a tangle but somehow managed to keep it out
on a length and outside off; Tewatia sits down and looks to go over the leg-side; misses as the ball goes past the outside-edge. Good comeback from Ashwani..
134.7ks good length ball outside off; Tewatia slices it fine and picks out third man
slower bouncer but it is too short and well over Shahrukh's head; called wide for height
very full delivery on leg; Shahrukh clears his front leg and clips it to deep square leg

MI lose the review. Quinton de Kock was super confident. As soon as the wide was signalled; he wanted it reviewed. Hardik too didn't waste much time and challenged the onfield; only for them to lose it. Short ball outside off; Tewatia watches it sail over him. Clearly over the head and the square leg umpire got it spot on

ball_event: out Caught by Quinton de Kock!! Well
event_info: well! The extra ball brings about a wicket. Everything going MI's way tonight. This is such a soft dismissal. Good length ball around off; Tewatia tries to be cheeky and attempts the steer to third man; but gets a thick outside-edge and Quinton de Kock bends low to his left; hangs in with both hands. Third man was brought inside the circle and Tewatia wanted to take advantage of that. Rahul Tewatia c Quinton de Kock b Ashwani Kumar 8(11) [4s-1]
nice and slow close to off; Shahrukh eases it wide of cover
78.1ks flighted and drifting in; Rashid leans forward and blocks
86.9ks tad short and sliding in; Rashid kneels down and sweeps it to deep square leg; easy brace
this time Rashid sweeps backward of square and picks out Ashwani in the deep; just a single
uhhh! Floated up teasingly and Shahrukh looks to drive through the off-side; the ball dips and drifts in; takes the inner part and rolls towards long-on
comes down the track and drags the drive off the inner half to long-on
134.8ks back of a length delivery; Rashid knocks it to the right of Ashwani

ball_event: out Caught by (sub)Raj Bawa!! Third wicket for Ashwani. What an inclusion he has been! Gujarat Titans' wheels are coming off. They are seven down and staring down the barrel. Rashid charges down and is met by a shortened length
event_info: not much width as well to free his hands; looks to slap it over cover and mistimes it completely. The ball goes towards cover and Bawa takes it comfortably over his head. Rashid Khan c (sub)Raj Bawa b Ashwani Kumar 4(6)
132ks back of a length delivery; Rabada defends it with a straight bat towards cover
135.1ks on a length and around off; Rabada eases it to mid-off
good length ball close to off; Rabada pushes it square of the wicket

ball_event: out Caught by Naman Dhir!! Ashwani Kumar has a 4-fer. He's playing his first game of the season and doing a great job. 122.5ks short of length delivery
event_info: Shahrukh did not spot the change in pace; he was early into the pull and mistimes it towards deep backward square. Dhir has been on fire in the field and he picks up his third catch; a simpler one compared to his previous catch. Gujarat Titans are on the verge of a big defeat here. Shahrukh Khan c Naman Dhir b Ashwani Kumar 17(13) [4s-1 6s-1]
well-directed short ball from Bumrah; angled it in from round the wicket and Rabada had nowhere to go. He fends it uppishly and the ball lands safely on the off-side
slanted down leg; Rabada helps it along to fine leg
115.6ks dipping slower ball; Ashok digs it out to mid-wicket
131.6ks short and angled down leg; Ashok gets inside the line and misses the tuck; called a wide
134.4ks full and attacking the stumps; Ashok brings his bat down and turns it backward of square leg
slower ball angled into the pads; Rabada clips it to deep square leg

that's a snorter. 127.7ks short ball; headed right towards the batter; Ashok takes his eyes off the ball; leaves his hands and bat hanging up; the ball thuds into the glove and lobs over the vacant short leg region
back of a length delivery; Rabada swings across the line and misses; the ball just goes over the stumps
smashed! This time Rabada connects. Almost similar sort of delivery to the previous one; but Rabada goes down the ground with his flat-batted hit; got it off the middle and beat long-on comfortably
132.4ks low full toss on leg; Rabada moves away and jams it onto his pad; the ball rolls back down the pitch

hammered! Length delivery; Ashwani rolled his fingers over the ball; Rabada stays leg-side and has the space to open his arms; thumps it flat and wide of mid-off. Hardik was inside the ring and he had no chance
133.9ks short of length outside off; Rabada is early into the pull and does not connect

in the air and safe. 116.5ks slower short of length delivery; Rabada pulls it in front of square and wide of deep mid-wicket. The fielder gets there on the bounce. Ashwani finishes a fine spell. His figures read - 4-0-24-4
carrom ball drifting in; Rabada prods forward and ekes out an inside-edge onto the pad

ball_event: out Stumped!! Ghazanfar has his wicket. He too has bowled well like the rest of his team mates. Rabada wasn't going to defend two balls in a row. He decides to come down the track
event_info: gets deceived in length but still goes through with the big shot; the ball beats the inside-edge and de Kock does the rest behind the stumps. Mumbai Indians are one wicket away from an impressive win. Kagiso Rabada st Quinton de Kock b AM Ghazanfar 12(14) [4s-2]
tossed up around off; Siraj leans forward and defends it to cover
carrom ball outside off; Siraj does not pick it and plays down the wrong line; the ball beats the outside-edge
slider down leg; Siraj moves inside the line and misses the glance; wide called

ball_event: out Lbw!! Mumbai Indians win by 99 runs. As emphatic as it gets. Four consecutive defeats
event_info: a poor record against Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad; and the odds stacked against them - but they’ve responded in dominant fashion; the way they often do. Siraj was done prodding and defending. He went for a big swipe across the line; missed the carrom ball; and was struck plumb on the back thigh right in front of the stumps. He opted for the review; but ball-tracking showed three reds - no doubt about it. Mohammed Siraj lbw b AM Ghazanfar 0(3)
